findings.description: N+1 query: monthly_revenue_report issues one order_items SELECT per paid order and aggregates in Python, when a single JOIN+SUM aggregate query would do it in one round-trip.

Non-sargable predicates: wrapping paid_at in strftime('%Y'/'%m') defeats any index on paid_at, forcing a full table scan whose cost grows with total order history; use a half-open paid_at range instead.
SELECT * in charge_order fetches every order column to use only discount_pct, wasting row-materialization I/O that grows as the orders schema widens.
Every public method opens and closes a fresh sqlite3 connection with no pooling/reuse, incurring per-call connection-setup overhead under web-service request load.
Out of perf scope but noted: full card number and CVV logged in plaintext at INFO level (PCI violation) — defer detail to critic-security.
